title: RoofTrace pipeline contract
description: Single source of truth for the request/response shapes that cross the Rails<->Python-sidecar boundary (ADR-008). All geographic coordinates at this boundary are WGS84 (EPSG:4326), [longitude, latitude] order, per the GeoJSON convention. Internally the sidecar reprojects to a local UTM zone for metric geometry (ADR-003); UTM never crosses this contract. Every measured entity carries `source` and `confidence` per the COMPANY.md honest-uncertainty rule and ADR-001. Blob payloads (point clouds, image tiles) never cross this contract inline: they are referenced by a Spaces object key (one bucket, prefixed per ADR-010), and the orchestrator mints a signed URL when a stage must fetch one.

Confidence.description: Model/measurement confidence in [0,1]. Surfaced to the user verbatim per the honest-uncertainty rule; never hidden.

GeometrySource.description: Provenance of a geometric value. `lidar`=public 3DEP LiDAR; `imagery`=satellite/aerial only; `fusion`=LiDAR+imagery combined; `capture`=iOS ARKit capture; `manual`=human override.

JobSpec.description: The unit of work Rails hands the sidecar: which address/parcel to measure.

job_id.description: Rails-side Job UUID; echoed on every response for correlation.

requested_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp when Rails enqueued the job.

Address.description: A geocoded address. `lon`/`lat` are WGS84; present once geocoding has resolved.

raw.description: The address string as the contractor typed it.

Polygon.description: A GeoJSON-compatible Polygon (RFC 7946). Coordinates are WGS84 [lon, lat]. Rings: first is the exterior, any subsequent are holes. Used for the building footprint and refined roof outline.

items.description: A linear ring: >=4 positions, first == last.

LiDARResult.description: Outcome of the LiDAR ingest stage. When `status` is LIDAR_MISSING the pipeline degrades to satellite-only per ADR-001; `point_array_ref` is then null.

status.description: Whether usable LiDAR coverage was found for the building.

point_array_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the cropped NumPy point array in local UTM; null when LIDAR_MISSING.

point_count.description: Number of points after the building crop; null when LIDAR_MISSING.

work_unit.description: USGS 3DEP work-unit metadata the points were sourced from.

epsg.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS the points were reprojected to.

Facet.description: One planar roof face. Vertices are WGS84 [lon, lat] tracing the facet outline; metric values (area, pitch) are derived in local UTM and reported in the documented units.

pitch_ratio.description: Rise-over-run, expressed as rise per 12 of run (e.g. 6.0 means 6/12).

pitch_degrees.description: Slope angle from horizontal, in degrees.

area_sq_ft.description: True (slope-adjusted) facet area in square feet.

Feature.description: A detected roof feature (VLM). `bbox_norm` is normalized [0,1] image coordinates against the satellite tile the detection was run on.

bbox_norm.description: Normalized bounding box [x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max] in [0,1] image space.

verified.description: Whether the second VLM verification pass (ADR-006) confirmed the detection.

Measurement.description: The unified result the orchestrator assembles and persists as a Rails Measurement row. Composes the per-stage outputs.

total_area_sq_ft.description: Sum of facet areas; null if not computable.

PipelineRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: run (or validate) the measurement pipeline for a job.

pipelineSchemaVersion.description: Schema version the caller serialized against; the sidecar rejects a mismatched major/minor.

PipelineResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the assembled measurement (or, for the no-op validate endpoint, a fixture echo).

status.description: OK=full result; PARTIAL=degraded (e.g. LiDAR-missing satellite-only); FAILED=unrecoverable.

RenderImageRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: render basemap/satellite tile images for the PDF (ADR-014). Prerenders so Grover doesn't fetch tiles at PDF time.

bbox.description: WGS84 bounding box [min_lon, min_lat, max_lon, max_lat].

image_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `artifacts/` prefix) of the rendered PNG.

FuseCaptureRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: ICP-align an iOS ARKit capture to the public-LiDAR points and re-run the plane fit.

capture_mesh_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `uploads/` prefix) of the uploaded ARKit world-mesh bundle.

icp_rmse_m.description: ICP alignment residual RMSE in meters; the fusion-quality number the capture-fusion acceptance demands.

arkit_to_utm.description: Row-major 4x4 transform from the ARKit capture frame into the local UTM CRS the points are in (the composition of the GPS seed projection and the ICP alignment). Persisted on convergence so later photo projection reuses the solved fusion transform rather than re-solving. Null on non-convergence.

utm_epsg.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS `arkit_to_utm` maps into (e.g. 32614); null on non-convergence.

ProjectPhotoRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: project measured facets onto a captured photo via pinhole projection with z-buffer occlusion for the AR overlay.

photo_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `uploads/` prefix) of the captured photo.

world_mesh_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `uploads/` prefix) of the ARKit world-mesh bundle, so the projector can recompute the camera-to-UTM transform from the mesh as a fallback when `arkit_to_utm` is absent.

arkit_to_utm.description: Row-major 4x4 transform from the ARKit capture frame into the local UTM CRS, carried forward from the fusion stage so the projector reuses the solved fusion transform. Null/absent => recompute from `world_mesh_ref` as a fallback.

utm_epsg.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS `arkit_to_utm` (and the facet vertices' reprojection) use.

pose_confidence.description: Confidence in the camera pose for this photo, carried forward so the projector can flag a low-confidence overlay.

camera_pose.description: Camera intrinsics + extrinsics captured alongside the photo.

features.description: Detected roof features to project alongside the facets, so the overlay can pin features onto the photo.

overlay_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `artifacts/` prefix) of the rendered overlay PNG.

composite_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `artifacts/<job_id>/projected/` prefix) of the rendered photo+overlay composite PNG.

overlay_svg_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `artifacts/<job_id>/projected/` prefix) of the vector (SVG) overlay, for crisp scaling in the viewer.

pose_confidence.description: Confidence in the camera pose used to project this overlay; surfaced verbatim per the honest-uncertainty rule.

occluded_facet_ids.description: facet_ids fully behind a nearer surface in the z-buffer (not visible in this photo), so the consumer can dim or omit them.

RenderEvidenceThumbnailsRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: render normalized evidence thumbnails from a job's capture photos for the report's on-site-evidence section. Photos are referenced by Spaces `uploads/` key; the sidecar writes the thumbnails under the `artifacts/<job_id>/evidence/` prefix.

photo_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `uploads/` prefix) of the source capture photo.

sequence_index.description: Capture order from the session manifest; the thumbnails are emitted in this order.

caption.description: Optional human-readable caption for the thumbnail (e.g. the guided-prompt label).

RenderEvidenceThumbnailsResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the rendered evidence thumbnails (under `artifacts/<job_id>/evidence/`), in `sequence_index` order.

thumbnail_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `artifacts/<job_id>/evidence/` prefix) of the rendered thumbnail.

sequence_index.description: Capture order this thumbnail corresponds to.

caption.description: Caption echoed from the request (or derived).

SourceAttribution.description: Provenance of the external data a pipeline stage used, so downstream surfaces (viewer, PDF, JSON export) can render the attribution the data licenses require (ROADMAP 'License & attribution'). Every stage that touches an external source returns one.

license.description: License or attribution string the source requires.

url.description: Canonical URL for the source or its license.

retrieved_at.description: When this data was fetched (or served from cache).

ResolveAddressRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: geocode an address and resolve its building footprint(s) + parcel.

address.description: The address string as the contractor typed it.

ResolveAddressResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the geocoded location, building polygon(s), and parcel boundary. All polygons WGS84. `parcel_polygon` is null when Regrid is unavailable/uncovered (the pipeline degrades gracefully); an empty `building_polygons` array is a hard failure surfaced as a 422 by the endpoint, not this shape.

parcel_polygon.description: Parcel boundary from Regrid, or null when unavailable/uncovered.

building_polygons.description: Building footprint(s) intersecting the parcel (or within the fallback radius). More than one => multi-building parcel; the orchestrator picks.

IngestLidarRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: fetch + crop the LiDAR point cloud for a building.

IngestLidarResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: outcome of the LiDAR ingest stage. Reuses the `LiDARResult` shape and adds the ingest-stage extras (the local UTM zone the points are in, their UTM bounds, warnings, attribution). When `lidar.status` is LIDAR_MISSING the pipeline degrades to satellite-only (ADR-001) and `utm_zone`/`bounds_utm` are null.

utm_zone.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS the cropped points are in (e.g. 32614); null when LIDAR_MISSING.

bounds_utm.description: [min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y] of the cropped points in the local UTM CRS (meters); null when LIDAR_MISSING.

LidarPointsRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: fetch a cached cropped LiDAR point array (the `point_array_ref` an ingest produced) and return it as browser-renderable WGS84 points for the interactive report overlay. The raw array is binary NumPy in local UTM (ADR-003); this stage decodes, downsamples, and reprojects it so UTM never crosses the contract. `building_polygon` is the same footprint the ingest used; the sidecar derives the local UTM zone from its centroid (the deterministic function the ingest used to reproject the points), so the zone need not be persisted on the Rails side.

point_array_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the cropped NumPy point array, from a prior IngestLidarResponse's `lidar.point_array_ref`.

max_points.description: Optional cap on returned points; the array is uniformly downsampled to at most this many. Null/absent uses the sidecar default cap.

LidarPointsResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the cached LiDAR points decoded, downsampled, and reprojected to WGS84 for the report overlay. `points` are [lon, lat, elevation_ft]; elevation is feet to match the report's units.

points.description: Roof points as [lon, lat, elevation_ft] in WGS84.

point_count.description: Total points in the cached array before downsampling.

returned_count.description: Number of points actually returned (after downsampling to the cap).

bounds.description: [minLon, minLat, maxLon, maxLat] of the returned points in WGS84; null when there are no points.

RefineOutlineRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: refine a footprint prior into a pixel-accurate roof outline via SAM2.

image_tile_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the satellite tile the prior sits on.

image_geo_bounds.description: WGS84 [west, south, east, north] geographic bounds of the image tile, to map pixels<->lon/lat.

RefineOutlineResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the refined roof outline. On a catastrophic SAM2 leak (IoU<0.5 vs prior) the stage returns the prior unchanged with a 'sam2_low_confidence' warning. `refined_polygon` WGS84 to match the input convention.

iou_with_prior.description: Intersection-over-union of the refined polygon vs the prior; a sanity number, not a confidence.

sam2_backend.description: Which inference backend produced this result (ADR-012).

RenderImageryRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: fetch a satellite tile covering a building's footprint and store it for the downstream SAM2 refine and VLM detect stages (the render-imagery stage). The sidecar owns all geospatial-data fetch incl. satellite imagery (ARCHITECTURE.md); the orchestrator feeds the returned `image_tile_ref` + `image_geo_bounds` straight into RefineOutlineRequest/DetectFeaturesRequest.

size_px.description: Target output edge size in pixels for the rendered tile.

target_gsd_m.description: Optional ground-sample-distance hint in meters/pixel; the sidecar picks the nearest available satellite resolution.

RenderImageryResponse.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the stored satellite tile for a building (the render-imagery stage). `image_tile_ref` + `image_geo_bounds` match the RefineOutlineRequest/DetectFeaturesRequest input convention so the orchestrator can pass them straight through.

image_tile_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the stored PNG tile.

image_geo_bounds.description: WGS84 [west, south, east, north] geographic bounds of the rendered tile, to map pixels<->lon/lat.

warnings.description: Non-fatal flags, e.g. 'naip_stale' (imagery older than expected for the region).

MeasurementGeometry.description: Sidecar -> Rails: the facet list + roll-up metrics produced by either the LiDAR plane-fit path or the no-LiDAR fallback path. The orchestrator folds this into the unified `Measurement`. Metric roll-ups are in the documented presentation units (sq ft, ft, degrees).

primary_pitch_ratio.description: Pitch (rise per 12) of the largest-area facet.

FitPlanesRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: RANSAC plane-fit the cropped LiDAR points into roof facets (LiDAR path).

point_array_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the cropped NumPy point array in local UTM (from the LiDAR ingest stage).

utm_zone.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS the points are in (echoed from the LiDAR ingest stage), so vertices can be transformed back to WGS84.

FallbackMeasurementRequest.description: Rails -> sidecar: the no-LiDAR fallback measurement — planimetric area from the refined outline divided by cos(inferred pitch). Produces a single-facet, lower-confidence, imagery-only measurement.

inferred_pitch_degrees.description: Pitch assumed from imagery heuristics (e.g. shadow/typical-for-region).

utm_zone.description: EPSG code of the local UTM CRS to compute planimetric area in.

DetectFeaturesRequest.description: Rails-internal request shape for the VLM feature detector. The detector lives in Rails (RubyLLM, ADR-006), not the sidecar, but its I/O is part of this contract so the orchestrator and JSON export share one shape.

image_tile_ref.description: Storage key (Spaces `cache/` prefix) of the satellite tile the detection runs on; `bbox_norm` on each Feature is normalized against this tile.

DetectFeaturesResponse.description: VLM feature-detection result. Each Feature's `source` is the `GeometrySource` enum (always `imagery` here — the detection is from the satellite tile); the model identity lives in the response-level `detector` field, NOT in the per-feature source enum, so geometry provenance and model provenance stay separate concerns.

detector.description: Identity of the model that produced these detections, e.g. 'gemini-flash-2.0'. Audit/provenance; surfaced to the user as the detection method.
